name: Stamina Movement
description: Stamina Movement is a character action that changes how the player moves through space.
design_notes: Stamina Movement should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For movement mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use stamina movement as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect stamina movement to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels directly connected to the avatar and able to express intent through motion.

parameters.name: movement_speed
parameters.description: Baseline travel speed while the mechanic is active.

parameters.name: acceleration
parameters.description: How quickly the actor reaches target speed.

parameters.name: turn_response
parameters.description: How quickly movement direction updates after input changes.

edge_cases: Stamina Movement is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Stamina Movement overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Stamina Movement runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Stamina Movement state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Stamina Movement plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Stamina Movement produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Stamina Movement strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Stamina Movement becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Stamina Movement with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Stamina Movement as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Stamina Movement with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Stamina Movement as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
